title: Toasts, Morph, Scramble, Swipe & Zero-JS Accordion
description: Five brand-new web platform primitives: @starting-style toasts, a View Transitions grid morph, text-decode scramble, accessible swipe actions, and a calc-size() accordion.

drops.title: Starting-Style Toast Choreography
drops.description: Toasts that animate in and out of display:none with zero animation JS, via @starting-style and allow-discrete.
drops.concept: The recently-Baseline @starting-style at-rule paired with transition-behavior: allow-discrete lets an element transition its own entrance the instant it appears in the DOM, and delays a display:none exit until its opacity/transform transition finishes. No JS timing hacks, no animation library.

interaction.mouse: Click a spawn button to add a toast; click × to dismiss early
interaction.click: Adjust duration and auto-dismiss sliders in the debug panel
interaction.touch: Same tap interactions work on mobile

drops.title: Shared-Element Grid-to-Detail Morph
drops.description: Tap a product tile and the native View Transitions API morphs it into the full detail card — same element, one function call.
drops.concept: Each grid tile and its matching detail card share a unique view-transition-name. Wrapping the DOM swap in document.startViewTransition() lets the browser auto-build a pseudo-element tree that morphs position, size and aspect-ratio between the old and new states, with unrelated siblings crossfading normally.
drops.source: Modern CSS Tools — View Transitions guide

interaction.mouse: Click any tile to expand into the detail view; click back to return
interaction.touch: Tap tiles to open, tap back to close

drops.title: Character-Lock Scramble Reveal
drops.description: A hacker-terminal decode effect where each character flickers through symbols until a sweeping per-character timer locks it in.
drops.concept: Every character in the target string gets its own lock-in time offset (index × charTimePerChar). Until that time passes, a shared interval swaps the character for a random symbol from a small charset; once elapsed, it locks to the true character permanently, producing a left-to-right decode sweep.

interaction.mouse: Adjust char speed and symbol flicker sliders, click Replay
interaction.click: Replay button re-triggers all three examples
interaction.touch: Debug panel hidden on small screens; effect auto-plays on load

drops.title: Accessible Swipe-to-Reveal Actions
drops.description: Drag a row to reveal archive/delete actions, with the same actions always reachable from a kebab menu so the gesture is never required.
drops.concept: Pointer Events (not touch-only listeners) track drag distance on a row's front layer, translating it via CSS transform proportional to a reveal-width threshold; crossing ~45% of that width on release snaps the row open, otherwise it springs shut. A persistent kebab-menu button exposes the identical actions for keyboard/non-pointer users.

interaction.mouse: Drag a row left to reveal actions, or use the ⋮ menu
interaction.click: Kebab menu → Archive/Delete, with Undo toast
interaction.touch: Swipe rows left/right on touch devices

drops.title: Zero-JS Animated Accordion
drops.description: Native <details> panels that animate to their true intrinsic height via calc-size(auto, size) — no wrapper div, no JS.
drops.concept: The ::details-content pseudo-element styles the expandable region of a <details> element directly. With interpolate-size: allow-keywords enabled, setting height: calc-size(auto, size) on the open state lets the browser transition to the box's real computed height instead of an approximated max-height, animating open/close with pure CSS.

interaction.mouse: Click any summary row to expand/collapse
interaction.touch: Tap summary rows on mobile; native <details> keyboard support included
